title: Ram Temple donation theft tests VHP’s demand to free temples from state control
content: Describing government control of temples as a ‘threat to Hindu society’, the VHP had launched a nationwide campaign in 2021 to free Hindu temples and religious institutions from government control

title: U.P. Congress delegation allowed to pray at Ram Temple after face-off with Ayodhya administration
content: “An impartial investigation is not happening,” State chief Ajay Rai said on the temple donations embezzlement case

title: INDIA bloc writes to CJI on ‘biased conduct’ of EC; DMK, AAP sign letter
content: The letter has been signed by 23 parties, including the AAP and DMK, which did not participate in the June 8 INDIA bloc meeting in Delhi; Jairam Ramesh says “Opposition parties are firmly anchored”

title: Ayodhya Bar boycott: Supreme Court has said even the ‘wicked’ have the right to a lawyer
content: A 2010 judgment of the Supreme Court, refers to Article 22(1) of the Constitution, which mandates that an arrested person should not be ‘denied the right to consult, and to be defended by, a legal practitioner of his choice’

title: U.P. Congress chief, MPs stopped from visiting Ram Temple in Ayodhya, put under house arrest
content: U.P. Congress chief Ajay Rai says he was arrested from his hotel and is being detained in a State-run university guest house; Amethi MP asks why the right to darshan is being denied on political grounds

title: Need for inclusive, integrated climate action
content: Climate and heat considerations should be integrated into occupational health policies for municipal and contracted sanitation workers

title: CBI arrests IAS official in Haryana govt. funds misappropriation case
content: CBI has so far filed charge-sheets against 17 accused in the Haryana case, including six bank officials of IDFC First Bank and AU Small Finance Bank, three public servants, two companies and six private individuals

title: Deep trust deficit stalling Siang mega dam in Arunachal Pradesh
content: Despite several potential benefits, local communities are opposing the subcontinent’s largest planned dam on the Siang river. Why is there resistance, and what does the government need to do build trust?

title: State orders twin probes into Davangere hospital oxygen cylinder blast
content: The State Health Department has ordered two separate inquiries into the oxygen cylinder explosion inside the operation theatre of the Chigateri District Hospital in Davangere

title: Jaishankar speaks with Ireland Foreign Minister, discusses ways to deepen ties
content: Ireland will take on one of the EU's most influential roles when it assumes the Presidency of the Council of the European Union from July 1 to December 31, 2026

title: Court convicts EMPEE’s Shaji Purushothaman in 2013 fatal drunk driving case
content: A 13-year-old boy was killed and five persons were injured in the accident involving the car Shaji drove. The prosecution alleged that none of the accused had attempted to aid the injured

title: India and Japan to renew focus on Indo-Pacific for the first visit by Prime Minister Takaichi
content: Ms. Takaichi’s visit, that marks the 20th annual summit since India and Japan begun to meet regularly at the highest levels and is expected to set the course for ties over the next decade

title: Criminal justice system’s digital push aims for a full roll-out by next year
content: Rollout of ICJS, which will integrate police, courts, prisons, forensics, and prosecution on one platform, will enable end-to-end digital workflow; data will be stored in government-owned cloud platform MeghRaj

title: Road restoration works planned across four GCC zones
content: Restoration works in areas such as Sholinganallur and Madipakkam, where multiple utility projects are under way, are expected to be completed by September, an official added.

title: Godrej Properties acquires 47-acre land parcel in South Chennai, eyes ₹500 crore revenue
content: Godrej Properties already has four developments in Tamil Nadu, excluding the project it has currently signed

title: ICEYE chooses India for sovereign SAR mission; picks Agnikul Cosmos as launch partner
content: Chennai-headquartered Agnikul Cosmos and ICEYE, the world leader in sovereign intelligence from space, have signed an MoU to explore the potential to build, launch and operate Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) earth observation systems from India

title: SpiceJet suspends flight services from city airport
content: The airline operated flights to cities including New Delhi, Ahmedabad, Kolkata, Patna, Hyderabad, and Shivamogga from Chennai; flight services are expected to resume in October this year

title: Tamil Nadu appeals to SC against Madras HC order on cow slaughter ban
content: The Madras HC order was based on the 1976 order which prohibited cow slaughter in the interest of milk production and improvement of rural economy.

title: One dead, 17 injured as naphtha pipeline catches fire in Bengal
content: Locals say that during the early hours of the morning there was an explosion with a loud sound in the Haldia Petrochemicals pipeline.

title: Former CM Naveen Patnaik hits out at Odisha govt, alleging ‘callous’ of handling of school textbooks
content: Demanding resignation of School and Mass Education Minister Nityanand Gond, BJD hit the street and charged massive embezzlement of government fund in printing of textbooks

title: Bodies of teenaged inter-caste lovers found in Mayiladuthurai district; violence erupts
content: Police launch probe amid suspicion of foul play and caste killing; pickets posted at the village and bodies shifted to Tiruvarur Government Medical College Hospital for autopsy

title: PDP says party ‘leaders prevented from protesting against outsourcing of govt. jobs in J&K’
content: PDP president Mehbooba Mufti said PDP leaders and workers across the Valley were placed under house arrest to prevent a peaceful protest against the outsourcing of government jobs

title: Indus Waters Treaty remains ‘valid, binding and operative’: Pakistan Deputy PM
content: Ishaq Dar claimed that any attempt to deprive Pakistan of the waters “rightfully allocated” to it would have “profound consequences” for regional peace and security, Radio Pakistan reported

title: Monsoon tracker updates: Monthly average rainfall over India expected to be below normal in July, says IMD
content: The flood situation worsened in Arunachal Pradesh, as one more person died, taking the toll to four, while 21 others were injured in the State, where 90,499 people were affected in almost all 28 districts, the authorities said on Tuesday.

title: TMMK protests over High Court order against quota for those who convert to Islam
content: TMMK president M.H. Jawahirullah appeals to the State govt. and the CM to take legal steps to nullify the verdict

title: Multi-agency mock drills focus on suicide attacks, calamities ahead of Amarnath yatra in Kashmir
content: The police said these drills were to assess emergency preparedness and response mechanisms ahead of the Amarnath Yatra, which will start from July 3

title: Guwahati police seize 37 kg gold from Maharashtra man
content: The bars of suspected precious metal, valued at ₹54 crore, are suspected to have been smuggled by an international group linked to Myanmar and West Asia

title: What is Centre’s new EPFO upgrade? | Explained
content: The plan is to modernise its service delivery mechanism through automation and rule-based processing to overcome technical and procedural delays in member services, such as the submission of claims for deceased members and the presentation of life certificates for pensioners

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay to hold consultative meeting with post-poll alliance partners on July 1
content: The meeting convened by the TVK assumes significance against the backdrop of the disintegration of the DMK-led Secular Progressive Alliance following the 2026 Assembly poll debacle.

title: Decks cleared for implementation of ₹12,642-crore VB-G RAM G in Tamil Nadu on July 1
content: As part of the funding arrangement, which is 60:40 for the Union and State governments, the former’s contribution will be ₹ 7,585 crore and the latter’s ₹ 5,057 crore, said an official in the State government.

title: Centre deputes Ministers to assess damage in flood hit Arunachal
content: Twelve of the State’s 28 districts have been affected; at least three people have died, roads, bridges, and houses have been damaged; Assam’s Dhemaji district has suffered too

title: Fourth accused arrested in Maharashtra TET scam
content: Remanded to police custody till July 6, the accused is the wife of the alleged kingpin of the scam, who is absconding; police say she was aware of the financial transactions in the multi-State racket, and had met the other accused

title: 'Ready for war': Iran draws red line for US, says no final talks until MoU terms are met
content: Iran's Parliament Speaker has issued a stark warning of war if the US fails to adhere to a memorandum of understanding, emphasizing that negotiations will halt until Washington fulfills its commitments.  Tehran views recent Persian Gulf incidents as violations, vowing a strong reaction. Discussions are currently focused on implementing the MoU, including the lifting of a naval blockade and the release of frozen assets, with technical talks planned in Doha.

title: Hardik Pandya moves base from Mumbai to Bengaluru: Here's why
content: Indian all-rounder Hardik Pandya has made a significant career shift, establishing his permanent training base at the BCCI's Centre of Excellence in Bengaluru. This move, driven by convenience and access to world-class facilities, marks him as the first high-profile active cricketer to do so. Pandya aims to continue playing international white-ball cricket for several more years, with his rehabilitation from injury ongoing.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has exited New York City real estate at a steep loss, selling his stake in struggling landlord A&E Real Estate for just six cents on the dollar. The Bloomberg-reported sale lands as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze on one million rent-stabilized apartments squeezes landlords already battered by 2019 rent laws, soaring costs and mounting unpaid rent.

title: '994 days of genocide': Microsoft engineer quits, emails thousands on his final day
content: A Microsoft data center technician in Italy resigned on his final day, emailing thousands of colleagues across Europe to accuse the company of powering Israel's war in Gaza through its Azure cloud and European data centers. Published by No Azure for Apartheid, the email cites the Unit 8200 surveillance exposé and urges Microsoft workers to "reclaim our labor" and divest.

title: Can Zohran Mamdani become US president? Here's what he said
content: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's political influence is growing, but a presidential bid remains out of reach. Despite meeting the age and residency requirements, the Constitution's natural-born citizen clause disqualifies him, as he was born in Uganda. Mamdani, however, expressed contentment with the current constitutional framework, emphasizing his focus on New York City.

title: Michael Dell gives update on Dell's plan to move company's base to Texas
content: Dell Technologies is officially moving its legal base from Delaware to Texas, with 97% shareholder approval. CEO Michael Dell expressed pride in returning the company to its founding state, highlighting Texas's role in its growth. This move follows a trend of major corporations relocating to Texas, attracted by its business-friendly climate.

title: Maple Leafs linked to blockbuster trade for Blue Jackets' Zach Werenski
content: The Toronto Maple Leafs are interested in trading for Columbus Blue Jackets’ defenseman Zach Werenski, who had a Norris Trophy-winning campaign, according to reports. The price for such a move would be massive, say NHL analysts, and Auston Matthews' friendship with Werenski has become another talking point surrounding Toronto's pursuit of the star defenseman.

title: ExtraEmily banned on Twitch after near-crash incident during livestream
content: ExtraEmily has been banned from Twitch after a June 28 IRL stream showed her nearly crashing while reading chat during a drive. The incident revived concerns about distracted driving and followed previous controversies, including a viewbotting dispute and an earlier driving-related suspension. The latest ban has again sparked debate over safety rules for livestreamers and the responsibility creators have while broadcasting in public.

title: Who are Erling Haaland's parents? Inside the Norway star's sporting family
content: Erling Haaland comes from one of Norway’s most accomplished sporting families. His father, former Premier League player Alf-Inge Haaland, and his mother, former Norwegian heptathlon champion Gry Marita Braut, both had elite athletic careers. The Manchester City striker is also close to his siblings Astor and Gabrielle Haaland, has two younger half-sisters, and has been in a long-term relationship with Isabel Haugseng Johansen, with whom he shares a son born in 2024.

title: Blake Lively reportedly unfazed after missing Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce invite
content: Reports claim Blake Lively is not expected to attend Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's upcoming wedding, reflecting the apparent distance in their once-close friendship. An anonymous source said Lively understands the decision and believes her presence would create a media frenzy due to her recent legal controversy. While she reportedly wishes to support the couple, neither Swift, Kelce nor Lively has publicly commented on the wedding rumors or the reported guest list.

title: Maple Leafs may have to sacrifice Matthew Knies to land Zach Werenski
content: Toronto Maple Leafs are reportedly eyeing Norris Trophy winner Zach Werenski from the Columbus Blue Jackets. Acquiring the star defenseman, however, could come at a steep price, potentially requiring the Maple Leafs to trade promising young forward Matthew Knies.  Columbus is seeking a significant return for their top blueliner, making this a potentially blockbuster offseason move.

title: Born in the USA: Supreme Court rebuffs Trump's bid to end birthright citizenship
content: The US Supreme Court has delivered a significant blow to President Trump's executive order, upholding birthright citizenship for children born on American soil to undocumented or temporary visa holders. In a 6-3 decision, the court affirmed that the 14th Amendment guarantees citizenship to nearly all individuals born in the U.S., reaffirming over a century of legal precedent. This ruling removes a key immigration initiative from the administration's agenda.

title: 40°C and counting: Europe's heatwave exposes a continent built for cold
content: Europe, once crafted to stay warm during harsh winters, now grapples with a grim new challenge: relentless heatwaves. Buildings that sheltered against frosty temperatures have turned into sweltering traps, revealing a serious flaw in adapting to intense summer heat. With infrastructure buckling under record temperatures and a surge in heat-related fatalities, a fundamental reevaluation of urban planning is desperately needed to face an increasingly hotter reality.

title: 'Stripped him, forced him to ... ': Temple priest 'abducted, assaulted' in Bangladesh
content: A Hindu law student and temple priest, Subhash Deuri, was allegedly abducted and assaulted in Dhaka, Bangladesh. Unidentified men held him captive, subjected him to torture, and forced him to arrange ransom payments. He was later found unconscious and hospitalized. Authorities are investigating the motive behind the incident, with no police complaint yet lodged.

title: 14 children killed as tuition centre roof collapses in Lahore
content: Tragedy struck Lahore as the roof of a private tuition centre, operating from an under-construction building, collapsed, claiming the lives of at least 14 young students aged seven to thirteen. Twenty others, including a teacher, were injured and hospitalized. Authorities have arrested the contractor as rescue efforts continue, with fears the death toll may rise.

title: Veteran Marathi theatre legend Vijaya Mehta passes away at 91
content: Veteran theatre director, actor and producer Vijaya Mehta, one of the most influential figures in modern Marathi theatre, passed away on Tuesday at the age of 91. She breathed her last at her residence, leaving behind a rich legacy that transformed the landscape of Indian theatre.

title: Morocco’s World Cup hero Ismael Saibari’s emotional hug with his mother wins hearts
content: Morocco's Ismael Saibari's emotional World Cup celebration with his mother resonated globally, highlighting a journey far beyond football. Born with a severe foot deformity, doctors doubted he would ever walk properly. His mother's unwavering faith and sacrifice saw him overcome this, leading to a moment of profound gratitude on the world stage. This touching scene underscores the power of parental belief in navigating life's most challenging obstacles.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for July 1
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for July 1, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: LeBron James trade: Five teams that could land the Lakers legend next
content: LeBron James has informed the Lakers that he plans to play elsewhere in the 2026-27 season, ending his eight-year run with the franchise. The Golden State Warriors are viewed as the early favorites, while the Cleveland Cavaliers, Miami Heat and Denver Nuggets are also being discussed. James is expected to prioritize a strong championship opportunity as free agency unfolds.

title: Shreya Reveals Akanksha's Secret: Lock Upp 2 contestant outs rival as bisexual
content: In a dramatic turn of events on Lock Upp 2, contestant Shreya Kalra has spilled the beans about Akanksha Chamola's alleged bisexuality to Sufi Motiwala as a calculated act of payback. This revelation follows Akanksha's public announcement of her divorce from Gaurav Khanna, who has been vocal about his unwavering love and support for her. Contestants are permitted to use such secrets strategically as 'lifelines' in the game.

title: Mumbai local stabbing: Viral video helps GRP recover knife used to kill 22-yr-old Mayank Lohar
content: A viral video proved crucial in recovering the knife used in a Mumbai local train stabbing. A hotel manager alerted police after his staff found the weapon, leading to its recovery. The accused, arrested for fatally stabbing a co-passenger after an argument, had his custody extended until July 6. Investigators are probing the motive and whether the knife was used previously.

title: England's Playing XI vs India in 1st T20I: Archer OUT; Buttler and Salt to open
content: England has unveiled their formidable playing XI for the opening T20I against India, captained by Harry Brook.  Boasting over 650 T20 international caps, the seasoned squad features a strong batting lineup including Phil Salt, Jos Buttler, and Brook himself.  With a balanced attack led by Saqib Mahmood and Adil Rashid, England aims to leverage their home advantage in the highly anticipated five-match series opener.

title: 'I didn’t play great in the Super Bowl': Sam Darnold echoes Jalen Hurts
content: Seattle Seahawks quarterback Sam Darnold expressed frustration over his Super Bowl performance, despite his team’s victory. He admitted he missed crucial throws. This mirrors the intense competitiveness seen in Philadelphia Eagles star QB Jalen Hurts. Even after winning a Super Bowl, Hurts was back training within weeks. An Eagles insider highlights that despite criticisms, Hurts consistently elevates his game in the postseason. The insider urges doubters to reconsider underestimating him.

title: Woman developed acute kidney injury after replacing meals with vegetable juices for weight loss
content: A homemaker's extreme diet, fueled by unregulated weight loss trends, led to acute kidney injury. For three months, she consumed massive amounts of green vegetable juices, causing oxalate crystals to damage her kidneys. Prompt diagnosis and a return to a balanced diet, alongside proper hydration, reversed the severe condition, highlighting the dangers of dietary extremes even with seemingly healthy foods.

title: Watch: Thief in ‘Modi mask’ steals smartphones from Rajasthan shop
content: Robbers stole between 35 to 40 smartphones from a Bhilwara mobile store, amounting to a staggering Rs 4-5 lakh. What’s peculiar is that CCTV footage shows one robber donning a mask of Prime Minister Narendra Modi during the operation. Interestingly, this same individual tried to break into another shop that same night.

title: Beth Mooney, Gardner power Australia into eighth Women's T20 World Cup final
content: Australia stormed into the ICC Women's T20 World Cup 2026 final, defeating West Indies by eight wickets in a dominant semi-final performance. After restricting the opposition to 125/7, Beth Mooney's unbeaten 61 and Ashleigh Gardner's quickfire 35 guided Australia to victory with ease. This marks their eighth appearance in the final as they chase a seventh title.

title: Scary scenes! Deandra Dottin carried off moments before Women's T20 World Cup semi-final
content: A scare rippled through the West Indies camp as star all-rounder Deandra Dottin was carried off before their T20 World Cup semi-final. Despite a medical issue, she bravely returned to score a crucial 26 not out, guiding her team to 125.  Australia, however, comfortably chased the target, securing an eight-wicket win and a spot in the final.

title: Kim Jong Un Vows To Deepen Ties With China In Message To Xi Jinping
content: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un vowed on Wednesday to keep strengthening relations with China, describing recent summit talks with President Xi Jinping in Pyongyang as a "historic occasion".

title: Trump Filing Shows He Took In About $1.2 Billion From Crypto Last Year
content: President Donald Trump took in nearly $1.2 billion dollars from his crypto businesses last year, a federal filing released Monday shows.

title: Delhi's Wrong-Side Driving Crackdown Intensifies; Over 2,000 FIRs In 6 Months
content: The data shows police issued 3,42,381 challans for wrong-side driving till June 25, compared with 1,72,879 during the same period last year- a 98% jump.

title: Rs 54 Crore Gold Haul: Assam Police Bust International Smuggling Racket
content: During the search, police recovered 37.064 kilograms of suspected gold, 13 grams of silver, four mobile phones, Rs 3,755 in cash, and bags allegedly used to conceal the consignment.

title: Russia Urges Pakistan, Afghanistan To Use Diplomacy To Solve Disputes
content: Russia's Foreign Ministry urged Pakistan and Afghanistan on Tuesday to use diplomacy to resolve their longstanding differences.

title: 3-Year-Old Rescued From Collapsed Building 6 Days After Venezuela Quakes
content: Venezuela was hit by two earthquakes of magnitudes 7.2 and 7.5 less than a minute apart last Wednesday, toppling buildings and trapping thousands of people beneath the rubble, according to authorities and rescue teams.

title: Israel Ex-Army Chief Launches Campaign In Bid To Unseat Netanyahu
content: Four months ahead of the planned polls, Gadi Eisenkot held his first electoral meeting, where he declared that "Israel deserves to open a new chapter. We will write it together."

title: Ready For War If Dialogue Fails: Iran Warns US Ahead Of Qatar Talks
content: Iran's chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf on Tuesday said that Iran was prioritising diplomacy with the United States, but remained ready for war.

title: Delhi Government Set To Launch EV Portal Soon. Here's What We Know
content: Under the new EV policy, verified applicants will receive purchase and scrapping incentives directly into their bank accounts via Direct Benefit Transfer.

title: Israel Won't Leave Lebanon As long As Hezbollah "Threatens Us": Netanyahu
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu visited troops in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, vowing that his country's forces would stay in the area as long as Iran-backed Hezbollah remained a "threat".

title: Assam Launches Drive Against Illegal Sawmills, Forest Minister Warns Of Action
content: The Forest Department has directed all Divisional Forest Officers (DFOs), Range Officers and field staff to conduct intensive inspections across every forest division.

title: "Civilians Suffering, Children Dying": Ukraine's Drone War Reaches Moscow
content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said the facility plays a role in intelligence operations and helps coordinate Russian military activities in Ukraine.

title: Explained: How Iran War Drove Up Costs And Shook Indian Markets
content: The Iran war has emerged as one of the defining geopolitical events of 2026, exposing several vulnerabilities in the Indian economy.

title: J&K Enforces Traffic Curbs For Amaranth Yatra. See Details
content: According to a traffic advisory, people have been asked to plan their journey accordingly and cooperate with the police

title: At Jaipur's Biggest Hospital, A Doctor's Retirement Put Transplants On Hold
content: With the media spotlight on the apathy of the hospital, the government has now swung into action, giving charge to a new doctor. The room 518 on the fifth floor of the hospital's Nephrology ward is now open.

title: Built Into BJP's Financial Push In Bengal - Calcutta Stock Exchange Revival
content: Founded in 1908, the Calcutta stock exchange was once India's second largest after the Bombay stock exchange. It thrived as much as the exchange in Mumbai, the country's commercial capital, or the one in Gujarat's Ahmedabad.

title: Karnataka To Review Free Power Scheme, Door-To-Door Verification From Tomorrow
content: The exercise will cover all beneficiaries of the Gruha Jyothi Yojane, introduced in July 2023 to provide up to 200 units of free electricity every month.

title: Bengal Removes 77 Muslim Communities From OBC List, Shrinks Quota
content: After the BJP government came to power in May, it said it would revoke the recognition of 77 Muslim communities that were given OBC status during the Trinamool rule. At the same time, OBC reservation would be reduced from 10 per cent to 7 per cent.

title: Ghalib Haveli, Malcha Mahal Among 75 Delhi Monuments Up For Adoption
content: The Delhi Cabinet on Monday approved two schemes that could change how many of these heritage sites are maintained.

content: BJP dismisses opposition's bid to revive its challenge to SIR by writing to the CJI as a diversionary tactic . the opposition keeps raising questions over the process even though SC has rejected pleas against the process and recognised EC’s power to do so .

content: Bharti Jain is senior editor with The Times of India, New Delhi. She has been writing on security matters since 1996. Her repertoire of news stories delves into the whole gamut of issues related to terrorism and internal strife, besides probing strategic affairs in India’s neighbourhood. Read More

title: Free navigation in Hormuz essential: PM Modi to Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian
content: govt said he reiterated the importance of freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz for India and the world . he called for lasting peace in an Indian readout . the readout said Modi welcomed the agreement between us and Iran .

content: attorney general R Venkataramani argued that 20% blending is a national policy . he urged SC to have all matters pertaining to the issue transferred to the court as adjudication of similar petitions were pending in various HCs .

title: SIR row escalates: 23 opposition parties write to CJI, question Election Commission's neutrality
content: DMK and AAP have signed the letter to the CJI despite not being part of the INDIA bloc . the two parties did not attend the meeting of the opposition alliance on June 8 . a joint letter signed by 23 political parties plus one independent has been sent to the cJI today .

title: Chinese imports, cyber security, AI: PM Modi flags concerns at cabinet secretary meet
content: steel and renewable energy ministries highlighted the need to protect domestic players from subsidised shipments . ministers said home secretary Govind Mohan stressed on cybersecurity systems and AI to avoid any foreign player weaponising them . steel secretary Sandeep Paundrik said that while steel consumption in India is increasing, there is a fall in demand in other countries, including China .

content: Lubna Kably is a senior editor, who focuses on various policies and legislation. In particular, she writes extensively on immigration and tax policies.
